And so there's two things that people do. The first thing is layer normalization and what layer normalization does is this is not so much for like gradient disappearing. It's more for preventing gradients from exploding or becoming very unstable.
And the way it works is it normalizes outputs to be within a consistent range, uh, preventing too much variance in the scale.
So, uh, the way layer number looks like is this. And, um, it's not too complicated, but it's a little bit of a complicated equation. So I'll go through it one at a time.
The first thing is we take the mean of the vectors. So we just add up all of the vectors and we divide by the number of vectors that we have here.
The next thing is the standard deviation of the vector. So we add up the value minus the mean squared and then take the square root of it, just stand normal standard deviation.
